changes.reason: Batch G fallback/1DTE-only adaptation. The 1DTE profile is profitable but tail-heavy with -375.7 worst MAE and only 167.9 PnL/exposure hour; raising the ES VWAP slope from 0.01 to the first grid level 0.04 tests whether weaker drift entries are causing the longer holds without touching VALID_0DTE profiles.

Keep the 1DTE fallback profile structurally aligned with the valid no-0DTE fallback standards. Current 1DTE trades are positive but average MAE is -123.1; requiring structure score 4 should remove weaker continuation attempts while preserving the profile's breakout thesis.

Current 1DTE profile uses a 3 point TP but lacks the max-distance cap from the Batch G grid. Setting max distance to 6 keeps target expansion available while limiting overwide dynamic targets that can extend exposure in 1DTE-only states.

Keep 1DTE flow confirmation intact but test a slightly stricter forward DEX ratio from 0.05 to 0.08. This targets the 20-trade, profitable but lower-efficiency 1DTE slice without changing option-confirmed 0DTE profiles or no-0DTE ES fallback profiles.

expected_effect: Should improve 1DTE-only risk-adjusted quality by reducing weak-structure, low-slope breakout entries, lowering average/worst MAE and exposure hours. The tradeoff is modest 1DTE trade-count and PnL risk; reject if total net profit drops below 98% of run_001, 1DTE PnL/exposure does not improve, or trade count falls more than 10% without materially better objective.
label: batch-g-1dte-quality-tighten
rationale: Follow Batch G using only routed fallback profiles. The latest run shows VALID_1DTE_ONLY contributes meaningful PnL, so it should not be disabled, but it is less efficient than the main VALID_0DTE pullback edge and carries -375.7 worst MAE. This is a profile-local quality test, not a global veto or stop-loss proxy, and it avoids tuning option-dependent 0DTE profiles for stale/missing data.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: breakout_long_1dte produced 20 trades, 1298.7 PnL, 167.94 PnL/exposure hour, 23.2 minute average hold, and -375.7 worst MAE. Removing it would retain only about 81% of current PnL, so the right test is quality tightening rather than deletion.
strategy_hypotheses.name: 1DTE Breakout Quality Gate
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Small sample and all trades are winners; stricter slope/structure/forward-DEX may remove profitable 1DTE insurance without enough MAE improvement to compensate.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: VALID_1DTE_ONLY routing where 0DTE is unavailable but a 1DTE breakout map exists.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: 1DTE PnL/exposure hour and average MAE improve, total net profit remains at least 98% of run_001, and VALID_0DTE performance is unchanged.

new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Pin Fast Recovery Split
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE and hold-time tail while preserving at least 85-90% of its PnL and keeping median hold near current 5.3 minutes.

new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Incremental 1DTE PnL/exposure hour above 150 with no degradation of breakout_long_1dte PnL and worst MAE no worse than -375.7.

new_strategy_ideas.name: Dormant Mean Reversion Probe
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 10 trades in replay/backtest, positive PnL/exposure hour above 150, average MAE below current -99.5, and no new worst-MAE record.

new_strategy_ideas.name: Delayed Profit Failure Tightener
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Lower average MAE and exposure hours with no more than 5% reduction in net PnL; specifically reduce 60-180 minute hold bucket and tail MAE.

new_strategy_ideas.name: Call Wall Acceptance Expansion
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Generate at least 5-10 trades while maintaining PnL/exposure hour above 250 and worst MAE materially below pullback/charm tails.

next_experiments.config_bias: Config-only activation of dormant pullback_long_1dte through the 1DTE router, with conservative profile gates and no changes to VALID_0DTE profile behavior.
